behavior_summary: Provides the runtime logic for interacting with a stair item: reading the stair's position and state, deciding a movement direction/orientation from the stair's type/frame/z bits, testing whether the destination tile is free/legal, and initiating the move or reporting failure.

Implements two cooperating processes: U8P_STAIR::STAIR::0080 which executes a single immediate stair interaction (plays entry SFX, performs legality tests, and spawns a short-lived helper on success) and U8P_STAIR::STAIR::0192 which monitors the stair, sets a global blocked/unblocked flag, plays feedback SFX, and spawns STAIR::0080 when a move should proceed.
Uses asynchronous patterns: suspend/loop and spawn of helper processes (FREE::1C19 and STAIR::0080) so the visible movement/animation and freeing are handled outside the immediate decision code.
notable_control_flow: Entry triggers interpret the actor entering the stair's trigger region or activating the stair; the code runs as a process that can suspend and be resumed.
Branching heavily depends on item state bits (Item::getZ low bits), item type (Item::getType), and frame/orientation (Item::getFrame) to select candidate destination frame/direction bytes.

Destination legality is decided by calling FREE::2411 and, in some branches, Item::legal_move. On success the code spawns FREE::1C19 (helper) and/or U8P_STAIR::STAIR::0080; on failure it plays a failure SFX and sets or leaves the hill-stair blocked state.
U8P_STAIR::STAIR::0192 sets/clears the global hanoiStairBlocked flag (0 = unblocked, 1 = blocked) to serialize/guard stair usage based on the test results.
open_questions: Exact numeric mapping of specific frame values (e.g., 0x62, 0x70, 0x63, 0x64) to world directions/orientations — code chooses these but their concrete meaning in world coords is inferred.
Who else in the system reads hanoiStairBlocked and how that flag coordinates with other gameplay systems (the routines here only write it).
Precise behavior performed by the spawned FREE::1C19 helper (likely free/animation/delayed effect) — its full semantics are inferred but not proven here.
Precise trigger/event that launches these processes (enterFastArea vs. explicit use) and whether they can be re-entered concurrently in unusual cases.

main_function.name: U8P_STAIR::0080
main_function.purpose: Execute a single stair interaction for a given stair Item*: play an entry sound, read the item's x/y/z/type/frame, compute a candidate destination orientation and coordinates, test destination availability (FREE::2411 and optionally Item::legal_move), spawn a short helper process on success to perform the follow-up (animation/free), or play a failure sound and suspend/loop on failure.
entry_conditions: Activated when the actor/owner interacts with or enters the stair's trigger region (inferred from naming and immediate SFX + suspend behavior).
Runs per-activation as a process that can suspend, so it reacts to the stair item's current state at time of activation.

function_map.name: U8P_STAIR::0080
function_map.role: Immediate stair interaction executor: decides target frame/direction from item state, tests destination, spawns helper processes to realize movement or plays failure SFX.

function_map.name: U8P_STAIR::0192
function_map.role: Higher-level stair monitor/orchestrator: repeatedly examines the stair state, runs availability/legal checks for candidate destinations, sets/clears the hanoiStairBlocked flag, plays feedback SFX, and spawns U8P_STAIR::STAIR::0080 when a move should proceed.

function_map.name: U8P_STAIR::playSFX
function_map.role: Play sound effects for success/failure/entry feedback during stair interactions.

function_map.name: Item::* (getX,getY,getZ,getType,getFrame,grab,legal_move,destroy,bark)
function_map.role: Item API used to read item coordinates and state, lock/grab the item prior to testing/moving, and in other modules to report or destroy items.

function_map.name: FREE::2411
function_map.role: Primary world/tile availability checker used to determine if the target world point is free for the move.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned short-lived helper (likely handles freeing, animation, or delayed follow-up) after a successful move test.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the immediate stair executor when a monitored stair state indicates a move should proceed.

external_calls.purpose: Play entry or failure sound to give audio feedback on attempted stair interaction.
Read the stair item X coordinate to build candidate destination WorldPoint(s).
Read the stair item Y coordinate to build candidate destination WorldPoint(s).
Read Z/elevation/packed flags; low bits determine branch selections for direction/frame.
Select stair subtype behavior and map frames to target directions.
Use the current frame/orientation to compute the destination frame/dir byte.
Lock or prepare the item before testing moves and potentially moving it.
Test whether the computed destination WorldPoint is free/available.
Perform a secondary/more stringent legality check for a candidate destination in some branches.
Spawn a helper process to carry out freeing, animation, or follow-up after a successful move test.
Probe candidate tiles to determine whether the stair is blocked and whether to spawn STAIR::0080.
Play success or failure feedback when monitoring stair availability.
Spawn auxiliary helper following monitoring decisions (similar role to STAIR::0080's spawn).

writes.name: hanoiStairBlocked
writes.likely_effect: Marks the stair as blocked (1) or unblocked (0) based on destination availability tests; used to guard/serialize stair usage by other systems.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the runtime logic for interacting with a stair item: reading the stair's position and state, deciding a movement direction/orientation from the stair's type/frame/z bits, testing whether the destination tile is free/legal, and initiating the move or reporting failure.
